nodes.label: AI for generating practice materials
nodes.label_negative: AI as fully autonomous material creator
nodes.grounding_span: I almost always aim for a comprehensive prompts which I then use as a base to do my own work with

nodes.label: AI as comprehension aid for unfamiliar topics
nodes.label_negative: AI-generated comprehension questions as rote and templated
nodes.grounding_span: I usually find the questions generated by AI are too rote or just too repetitive/templated

nodes.label: AI as brainstorming and idea generator
nodes.label_negative: AI for culturally irrelevant or inappropriate ideas
nodes.grounding_span: I really like AI for actual idea generation, especially when it comes to finding either age-appropriate or culturally-relevant topics

nodes.label: AI for language translation
nodes.label_negative: AI translation inaccuracy for Korean nuances
nodes.grounding_span: trying to provide translations in Korean of something, because a lot of Korean includes their emotions/opinions built into the grammar

nodes.label: AI subscription cost vs. usage frequency
nodes.label_negative: AI subscription cost justified by frequent use
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think I use AI enough to justify the cost, especially when I'm still trying to pay off my grad school debt

nodes.label: AI usage influenced by older coworkers' traditional methods
nodes.label_negative: AI usage influenced by tech-savvy peers
nodes.grounding_span: most of my coworkers are significantly older than me, so their reactions to AI usage are usually either awe or a request for me to teach them how to use it

nodes.label: Authentic language learning experience for students
nodes.grounding_span: I'm more focused on getting authentic language experiences to them

nodes.label: Professional judgment and expertise
nodes.grounding_span: I'll make my own comprehension questions, because I usually find the questions generated by AI are too rote

nodes.label: Student engagement and learning
nodes.grounding_span: which really got my students excited

nodes.label: Efficiency and reduced workload
nodes.grounding_span: It seriously reduced my workload a lot

nodes.label: Financial prudence
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think I use AI enough to justify the cost, especially when I'm still trying to pay off my grad school debt

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for planning
nodes.grounding_span: Probably my greatest success with AI at work came from generating a summer-camp plan focused around the theme of recycling and saving the earth

nodes.label: Frustration with AI translation inaccuracies for Korean
nodes.grounding_span: I don't have too many experiences of being frustrated, but I think every one of them came with trying to provide translations in Korean

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for comprehension
nodes.grounding_span: I usually find the questions generated by AI are too rote or just too repetitive/templated

nodes.label: Preference for human handling of creative tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I like to do any image sourcing or visually creative tasks myself

nodes.label: Openness to increasing AI use in future
nodes.grounding_span: I imagine that as the cross-language usage becomes less risky, I'll probably have more use for it

nodes.label: Iterative back-and-forth prompting
nodes.grounding_span: I almost always aim for a comprehensive prompts which I then use as a base to do my own work with

nodes.label: Systematic cross-referencing — uses AI as starting point then verifies with multiple sources
nodes.grounding_span: I look up the topics presented on something like Naver, Daum, or NamuWiki
